description: Comprehensive macOS monitoring dashboard for node-exporter with system metrics: CPU (usage, per-core, load), Memory (composition, swap, pressure), Disk (IO, IOPS, latency), Network (throughput, Wi-Fi, errors), Battery, advanced Wi-Fi (darwin_wifi/darwin_wdutil)

panels.description: Sustained saturation marker: >0.7 for 5-10+ minutes usually means CPU-bound load; short spikes are normal.
panels.title: CPU Usage

panels.description: If this rises while used RAM stays flat, the system is shifting into compression/pressure; sustained >0.6 often causes latency spikes.
panels.title: Memory Pressure

panels.description: On APFS, snapshot headroom matters: below ~15% free space, latency and background stalls typically increase sharply.
panels.title: Disk Usage

panels.description: Read together with Power Draw and Health: fast drain at moderate draw often points to battery degradation.
panels.title: Battery

panels.description: Baseline for load normalization: load1 around core count means full utilization without deep queueing.
panels.title: CPU Cores

panels.description: Context for percentage memory metrics; mostly static by itself and useful for trend comparisons.
panels.title: RAM Total

panels.description: Swap alone is not critical; risk starts when swap size grows together with active swap in/out.
panels.title: Swap Used

panels.description: On APFS this can drop in steps due to snapshots; correlate with Disk Busy and Await.
panels.title: Filesystem Available

panels.description: If load is above core count while CPU% is moderate, the bottleneck is usually I/O, lock contention, or scheduler wait.
panels.title: System Load

panels.description: After reboot, baselines shift (cache, thermal state); compare anomalies within the same uptime window.
panels.title: Uptime

panels.description: Look for persistently hot cores: a sign of affinity/pinning or uneven interrupt distribution.
panels.title: CPU Usage Heatmap (per core)

panels.description: Rising system/iowait with low user share usually indicates a kernel/I/O bottleneck, not CPU-bound application code.
panels.title: CPU Modes

panels.description: Divergence between load1 and load15 helps separate brief bursts from sustained degradation.
panels.title: System Load

panels.description: Large per-core spread under similar workload suggests pinning, IRQ imbalance, or background daemon spikes.
panels.title: CPU per Core

panels.description: Rising Compressed with stable Active/Wired means pressure is absorbed by compression rather than memory release.
panels.title: Memory Composition

panels.description: If Memory Pressure grows faster than Allocated, reclaim/compression is the problem, not just higher occupancy.
panels.title: Memory Usage %

panels.description: Gradual swap growth can be normal; step-like jumps usually correlate with foreground-heavy sessions.
panels.title: Swap Usage

panels.description: Symmetric swap in/out bursts are a classic thrashing pattern and a common source of freezes/jitter.
panels.title: Swap Activity

panels.description: Low throughput with high busy/await usually means small random I/O or contention at FS/controller level.
panels.title: Disk IO Throughput

panels.description: High IOPS with low throughput indicates many small operations rather than sequential transfer.
panels.title: Disk IOPS

panels.description: Rising await without throughput growth typically means device queueing/wait, not CPU shortage.
panels.title: Disk Await Time

panels.description: Sustained queue depth >1 on desktop often correlates with UI lag and slow file-open latency.
panels.title: Disk Queue

panels.description: Sustained >0.8 indicates near device saturation; short spikes during backup/indexing are expected.
panels.title: Disk Busy %

panels.description: Track usage that does not return after cleanup; typical causes are snapshots, caches, or runaway logs.
panels.title: Filesystem Space

panels.description: Strong RX/TX asymmetry can be normal, but mismatch with expected traffic profile often indicates link constraints.
panels.title: Network Throughput

panels.description: Error growth without throughput growth usually points to link/driver issues rather than application behavior.
panels.title: Network Packets (RX+) and Errors (red)

panels.description: SNR <20 dB usually yields unstable throughput; rising noise at steady RSSI often means external interference.
panels.title: Wi-Fi Signal Quality

panels.description: MCS/width drops with normal RSSI usually indicate interference, DFS events, or airtime congestion.
panels.title: Wi-Fi Link Metrics

panels.description: Correlate with RSSI/SNR: higher traffic with falling SNR usually explains retries and latency growth.

targets.expr: sum by (instance, device) (irate(node_network_receive_bytes_total{instance="$instance"}[$__rate_interval]) * on(instance, device) group_left() label_replace(max by (instance, interface) (darwin_wifi_info{instance="$instance"}), "device", "$1", "interface", "(.*)"))

sum by (instance, device) (irate(node_network_transmit_bytes_total{instance="$instance"}[$__rate_interval]) * on(instance, device) group_left() label_replace(max by (instance, interface) (darwin_wifi_info{instance="$instance"}), "device", "$1", "interface", "(.*)"))

panels.title: Wi-Fi Usage

panels.description: High CCA Busy with good RSSI indicates a busy channel, not weak AP signal.
panels.title: wdutil Radio Metrics

panels.description: Faults without matching Recoveries suggest degrading radio/driver state and possible need for reinitialization.
panels.title: wdutil Fault and Recovery

panels.description: Use this to validate roaming behavior: frequent BSSID changes under one SSID often indicate roaming pressure and unstable performance.

targets.expr: (darwin_wifi_info{instance="$instance"} * on(instance, interface, account_id) group_left(bssid, ipv4_address, dns_server) (darwin_wdutil_wifi_info{instance="$instance"} or darwin_wdutil_info{instance="$instance"})) or (darwin_wifi_info{instance="$instance"} unless on(instance, interface, account_id) (darwin_wdutil_wifi_info{instance="$instance"} or darwin_wdutil_info{instance="$instance"}))

panels.title: Wi-Fi Connection Info

panels.description: If Health declines faster than charge level, real battery life is degrading even at the same percentage.
panels.title: Battery Level

panels.description: Useful idle indicator: sustained high draw often means wakeups, peripherals, or background workload.
panels.title: Battery Power Draw

panels.description: Absolute time is noisy; trend and degradation rate are more reliable than single readings.
panels.title: Battery Time Remaining

panels.description: Sustained high battery temperature accelerates wear; correlate with Power Draw and charging mode.
panels.title: Battery Temperature

panels.description: Voltage sag under load at stable temperature often indicates battery aging.
panels.title: Battery Voltage

panels.description: Transitions into serious/critical explain throttling and performance loss even when CPU% looks moderate.
panels.title: Thermal Pressure

panels.description: Comparing CPU/GPU/Disk temperature dynamics helps localize heat source and separate compute from I/O-driven load.
panels.title: Component Temperatures

title: MacBook
